reasoning: Google's "Year in Search" lists (Global → Trending → People) measure year-over-year spikes in search traffic. Joe Biden had an astronomical search presence in 2024 due to the election and his withdrawal from the race, which sets an incredibly high baseline. In 2025, while he had a significant news event (a Stage 4 cancer diagnosis in May), his overall search volume is highly unlikely to have spiked above 2024 levels, let alone enough to make him one of the top trending people globally. Additionally, current discussions of the most searched/trending people for 2025 focus on figures like Pope Leo XIV, Donald Trump, Bianca Censori, and Elon Musk, with Biden notably absent from top 10 projections. Finally, the resolution strictly requires him to be exactly #2, which further dramatically reduces the probability. Therefore, I estimate the probability at the minimum allowable floor.
